description: HP/Agilent 34401A 6.5-digit digital multimeter. Measures DC/AC voltage and current, 2- and 4-wire resistance, frequency, period, continuity and diodes, with math and limit-testing features. Programmed using SCPI over GPIB (IEEE-488) or RS-232.

identity.description: Standard SCPI identification query. Returns the multimeter's identification string (dimension a string of at least 35 characters).

commands.name: measure_dc_voltage
commands.description: Preset and make a DC voltage measurement with the specified range and resolution; the reading is sent to the output buffer.

parameters.name: range
parameters.range: 100 mV, 1 V, 10 V, 100 V, 1000 V; or MIN|MAX|DEF (DEF = autorange)

commands.name: measure_dc_voltage_ratio
commands.description: Preset and make a DC:DC ratio measurement. The specified range applies to the Input terminals; the reference (Sense terminals) is always autoranged.

commands.name: measure_ac_voltage
commands.description: Preset and make an AC voltage measurement. For AC, resolution is fixed at 6.5 digits; the resolution parameter only affects the front-panel display.

parameters.name: range
parameters.range: 100 mV, 1 V, 10 V, 100 V, 750 V; or MIN|MAX|DEF

commands.name: measure_dc_current
commands.description: Preset and make a DC current measurement with the specified range and resolution; the reading is sent to the output buffer.

parameters.name: range
parameters.range: 10 mA, 100 mA, 1 A, 3 A; or MIN|MAX|DEF

commands.name: measure_ac_current
commands.description: Preset and make an AC current measurement. For AC, resolution is fixed at 6.5 digits; the resolution parameter only affects the front-panel display.

commands.name: measure_resistance
commands.description: Preset and make a 2-wire ohms measurement with the specified range and resolution.

commands.name: measure_fresistance
commands.description: Preset and make a 4-wire ohms measurement with the specified range and resolution.

commands.name: measure_frequency
commands.description: Preset and make a frequency measurement. One 'range' covers all inputs between 3 Hz and 300 kHz; with no input applied the result is 0.

commands.name: measure_period
commands.description: Preset and make a period measurement. One 'range' covers all inputs between 0.33 s and 3.3 us; with no input applied the result is 0.

commands.name: measure_continuity
commands.description: Preset and make a continuity measurement. Range and resolution are fixed (1 kohm range, 5.5 digits).

commands.name: measure_diode
commands.description: Preset and make a diode measurement. Fixed at 1 Vdc range with 1 mA current source and 5.5 digits.

commands.name: configure_dc_voltage
commands.description: Preset and configure the multimeter for DC voltage measurements; does not initiate the measurement.

parameters.name: range
parameters.range: 100 mV to 1000 V; or MIN|MAX|DEF (DEF = autorange)

commands.name: configure_dc_voltage_ratio
commands.description: Preset and configure for DC:DC ratio measurements; does not initiate the measurement. Range applies to the Input terminals; reference is autoranged.

commands.name: configure_ac_voltage
commands.description: Preset and configure for AC voltage measurements; does not initiate. Resolution is fixed at 6.5 digits (parameter affects display only).

commands.name: configure_dc_current
commands.description: Preset and configure for DC current measurements; does not initiate the measurement.

commands.name: configure_ac_current
commands.description: Preset and configure for AC current measurements; does not initiate. Resolution fixed at 6.5 digits (parameter affects display only).

commands.name: configure_resistance
commands.description: Preset and configure for 2-wire ohms measurements; does not initiate the measurement.

commands.name: configure_fresistance
commands.description: Preset and configure for 4-wire ohms measurements; does not initiate the measurement.

commands.name: configure_frequency
commands.description: Preset and configure for a frequency measurement; does not initiate. One range covers 3 Hz to 300 kHz.

commands.name: configure_period
commands.description: Preset and configure for a period measurement; does not initiate. One range covers 0.33 s to 3.3 us.

commands.name: configure_continuity
commands.description: Preset and configure for continuity measurements; does not initiate. Fixed at 1 kohm range, 5.5 digits.

commands.name: configure_diode
commands.description: Preset and configure for diode measurements; does not initiate. Fixed at 1 Vdc range with 1 mA source and 5.5 digits.

commands.name: configure_query
commands.description: Query the multimeter's present configuration and return a quoted string.

commands.name: sense_function
commands.description: Select the measurement function. The function name must be enclosed in quotes, e.g. FUNC "VOLT:DC". Valid: VOLTage:DC, VOLTage:DC:RATio, VOLTage:AC, CURRent:DC, CURRent:AC, RESistance, FRESistance, FREQuency, PERiod, CONTinuity, DIODe.

parameters.name: function
parameters.range: VOLTage:DC | VOLTage:DC:RATio | VOLTage:AC | CURRent:DC | CURRent:AC | RESistance | FRESistance | FREQuency | PERiod | CONTinuity | DIODe

commands.name: sense_range
commands.description: Select or query the range for the selected function. For frequency and period use FREQuency:VOLTage:RANGe or PERiod:VOLTage:RANGe (range applies to input voltage, not frequency).

commands.name: sense_range_auto
commands.description: Disable or enable autoranging for the selected function. Thresholds: down-range below 10% of range, up-range above 120% of range. Returns 0 (OFF) or 1 (ON).

commands.name: sense_resolution
commands.description: Select or query the resolution for the specified function (not valid for frequency, period, or ratio). Specify in the same units as the function. MIN = best resolution, MAX = least resolution.

commands.name: sense_nplcycles
commands.description: Select the integration time in number of power line cycles for the present function (default 10 PLC). Valid only for DC volts, ratio, DC current, 2-wire and 4-wire ohms.

commands.name: sense_frequency_aperture
commands.description: Select the aperture (gate) time for frequency measurements (default 0.1 s). Choose 10 ms (4.5 digits), 100 ms (5.5 digits), or 1 s (6.5 digits).

commands.name: sense_period_aperture
commands.description: Select the aperture (gate) time for period measurements (default 0.1 s). Choose 10 ms (4.5 digits), 100 ms (5.5 digits), or 1 s (6.5 digits).

commands.name: sense_detector_bandwidth
commands.description: Specify the lowest frequency expected in the input signal; selects the slow, medium (default), or fast AC filter. Query returns +3.000000E+00, +2.000000E+01, or +2.000000E+02.

commands.name: sense_zero_auto
commands.description: Disable or enable (default) autozero mode. OFF defers a new zero until the next wait-for-trigger state; ONCE issues an immediate zero then behaves like OFF. Query returns 0 (OFF/ONCE) or 1 (ON).

commands.name: input_impedance_auto
commands.description: Disable or enable automatic input resistance for DC voltage. AUTO OFF (default) fixes input at 10 Mohm on all ranges; AUTO ON sets >10 Gohm on the 100 mV, 1 V, and 10 V ranges. Query returns 0 or 1.

commands.name: route_terminals
commands.description: Query whether the front or rear input terminals are selected. Returns FRON or REAR.

commands.name: calculate_function
commands.description: Select the math function (only one enabled at a time; default NULL). Query returns NULL, DB, DBM, AVER, or LIM.

commands.name: calculate_state
commands.description: Disable or enable the selected math function. Query returns 0 (OFF) or 1 (ON).

commands.name: calculate_average_minimum
commands.description: Read the minimum value found during a min-max operation. Cleared when min-max is turned on, at power-up, or after a remote reset.

commands.name: calculate_average_maximum
commands.description: Read the maximum value found during a min-max operation. Cleared when min-max is turned on, at power-up, or after a remote reset.

commands.name: calculate_average_average
commands.description: Read the average of all readings taken since min-max was enabled.

commands.name: calculate_average_count
commands.description: Read the number of readings taken since min-max was enabled.

commands.name: calculate_null_offset
commands.description: Store a null value in the Null Register (math must be on first). Range 0 to +/-120% of the highest range for the present function.

commands.name: calculate_db_reference
commands.description: Store a relative value in the dB Relative Register (math must be on first). Range 0 to +/-200 dBm (MIN=-200, MAX=200).

commands.name: calculate_dbm_reference
commands.description: Select the dBm reference resistance. Choices: 50, 75, 93, 110, 124, 125, 135, 150, 250, 300, 500, 600, 800, 900, 1000, 1200, or 8000 ohms (MIN=50, MAX=8000). Stored in non-volatile memory.

commands.name: calculate_limit_lower
commands.description: Set the lower limit for limit testing. Range 0 to +/-120% of the highest range for the present function.

commands.name: calculate_limit_upper
commands.description: Set the upper limit for limit testing. Range 0 to +/-120% of the highest range for the present function.

commands.name: data_feed
commands.description: Select whether readings taken via INITiate are stored in internal memory. "CALCulate" (default) stores up to 512 readings; "" disables storage (useful with min-max to avoid storing individual values). Query returns CALC or empty.

commands.name: initiate
commands.description: Change the trigger system from idle to wait-for-trigger. Readings are stored in internal memory (up to 512); retrieve them with FETCh?.

commands.name: read
commands.description: Change the trigger system from idle to wait-for-trigger and send readings immediately to the output buffer. Equivalent to INITiate followed by FETCh? but not buffered internally.

commands.name: fetch
commands.description: Transfer readings stored in internal memory by the INITiate command to the output buffer for reading into the bus controller.

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Select the trigger source: BUS (software), IMMediate (internal, default), or EXTernal (rear-panel Ext Trig). Query returns BUS, IMM, or EXT.

commands.name: trigger_delay
commands.description: Insert a trigger delay between the trigger and each sample. Range 0 to 3600 seconds (MIN=0, MAX=3600).

commands.name: trigger_delay_auto
commands.description: Disable or enable automatic trigger delay (determined by function, range, integration time, and AC filter). Setting a specific delay turns auto off. Query returns 0 or 1.

commands.name: sample_count
commands.description: Set the number of readings (samples) taken per trigger. Range 1 to 50000 (MIN=1, MAX=50000).

commands.name: trigger_count
commands.description: Set the number of triggers accepted before returning to idle. Range 1 to 50000; INFinite continuously accepts triggers (device clear to stop). Infinite query returns 9.90000000E+37.

commands.name: display_state
commands.description: Turn the front-panel display off or on. Query returns 0 (OFF) or 1 (ON).

commands.name: display_text
commands.description: Display a message on the front panel (up to 12 characters; extra characters truncated). Query returns the message as a quoted string.

commands.name: display_text_clear
commands.description: Clear the message displayed on the front panel.

commands.name: system_beeper
commands.description: Issue a single beep immediately.

commands.name: system_beeper_state
commands.description: Disable or enable the front-panel beeper (stored in non-volatile memory). Query returns 0 (OFF) or 1 (ON).

commands.name: system_error
commands.description: Query the error queue (up to 20 errors, FIFO order, each up to 80 characters).

commands.name: system_version
commands.description: Query the present SCPI version implemented by the multimeter.

commands.name: data_points
commands.description: Query the number of readings currently stored in internal memory.

commands.name: system_local
commands.description: Place the multimeter in local mode for RS-232 operation; all front-panel keys are fully functional.

commands.name: system_remote
commands.description: Place the multimeter in remote mode for RS-232 operation; all front-panel keys except LOCAL are disabled.

commands.name: system_rwlock
commands.description: Place the multimeter in remote mode with all front-panel keys disabled including LOCAL.

commands.name: status_questionable_enable
commands.description: Enable bits in the Questionable Data enable register; selected bits are reported to the Status Byte. Query returns the binary-weighted decimal of enabled bits.

commands.name: status_questionable_event
commands.description: Query the Questionable Data event register. Returns the binary-weighted decimal sum of all set bits.

commands.name: status_preset
commands.description: Clear all bits in the Questionable Data enable register.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clear the Status Byte summary register and all event registers.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Enable bits in the Standard Event enable register; selected bits are reported to the Status Byte. Query returns binary-weighted decimal of enabled bits.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Query the Standard Event register. Returns binary-weighted decimal of set bits.

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Set the operation complete bit (bit 0) in the Standard Event register after the command is executed.

commands.name: operation_complete_query
commands.description: Return 1 to the output buffer after the command is executed (used for synchronization).

commands.name: power_on_status_clear
commands.description: Power-on status clear. *PSC 1 clears the Status Byte and Standard Event enable masks at power-on; *PSC 0 preserves them. Stored in non-volatile memory. Query returns 0 or 1.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Enable bits in the Status Byte enable register. Query returns binary-weighted decimal of enabled bits.

commands.name: status_byte_query
commands.description: Query the Status Byte summary register. Similar to a serial poll but processed like any other command; the request-service bit (bit 6) is not cleared.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset the multimeter to its power-on configuration.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Perform a complete self-test. Returns 0 if successful, 1 if it fails.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Bus trigger. Triggers the multimeter when TRIGger:SOURce is set to BUS (equivalent to an IEEE-488 Group Execute Trigger).

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Read the multimeter's identification string (dimension a string of at least 35 characters).

commands.name: calibration_query
commands.description: Perform a calibration using the value set by CALibration:VALue. The multimeter must first be unsecured with the correct security code.

commands.name: calibration_count
commands.description: Query the number of times the multimeter has been calibrated (increments to 32767 then wraps to 0). Stored in non-volatile memory.

commands.name: calibration_secure_code
commands.description: Enter a new calibration security code (up to 12 characters). The multimeter must first be unsecured with the old code. Stored in non-volatile memory.

commands.name: calibration_secure_state
commands.description: Unsecure or secure the multimeter for calibration using the security code (up to 12 characters). Query returns 0 (OFF) or 1 (ON). Stored in non-volatile memory.

commands.name: calibration_string
commands.description: Record a calibration message (up to 40 characters; only 12 shown on the front panel). Can only be written from the remote interface. Stored in non-volatile memory.

commands.name: calibration_value
commands.description: Specify (or query) the value of the known calibration signal used by the calibration procedure.
